title: disanthus cercidifolius subsp. cercidifolius
intro: Disanthus cercidifolius subsp. cercidifolius, also known as the Japanese maplesquill or maple-leafed disanthus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hamamelidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Disanthus cercidifolius subsp. cercidifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, and its roots, stems, and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and other medicinal products.
